title: Oil just hit $98 a barrel and it has nothing to do with how much gas you're buying
text:
Oil broke past $98 a barrel, the highest it has been in years, after tankers were struck off Saudi Arabia and Trump escalated his threats against Iran.

Traders are now pricing in a real chance that a wider Middle East war shuts down the shipping lanes that move a huge share of the planet's oil. That fear is pushing the price up, not demand.

Every dollar oil climbs works its way into the price at your local gas pump. $100 oil used to be a crisis headline. Right now it is just the number the whole market is bracing for.
highlights: highest it has been in years
tankers were struck off Saudi Arabia
Trump escalated his threats against Iran
shipping lanes that move a huge share of the planet's oil
the number the whole market is bracing for
